_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 3 currently banned, 8772 total banned
ssh_attacks.change_since_last: fail2ban total bans 8772 (up from 8730, +42). 3 IPs currently banned. Currently banned: 2.57.121.25, 80.94.92.177, 213.209.159.159. Continued brute-force from 2.57.121.x subnet and new IPs 80.94.92.x, 213.209.159.x. Generic usernames only. No server-specific reconnaissance. No successful unauthorized logins.

pdf_security.document_write_safe: 1 usage in keyboard-shortcuts.js line 416 — writes to newly opened blank window for print cheat sheet only. All content escaped via escapeHtml(). Acceptable pattern.
pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 323 usages across 89 JS files (up from 318/88 — color-picker.js added 5).

color_picker_js.innerHTML_safe: Line 200: info panel template — all interpolated values are internally-computed: hex from rgbToHex (integer-derived #XXXXXX format), rgb/hsl/cmyk from conversion functions (integer-derived strings), pageNum (integer from DOM dataset), contrast ratios (number.toFixed(1)), wcagLevel (returns fixed strings 'AAA'/'AA'/'AA large'/'Fail'). formatRow() inserts hardcoded labels ('HEX','RGB','HSL','CMYK') and color format strings into data-value attributes — no user-controlled strings. Line 263: hardcoded HTML entity ★. Line 267: hardcoded HTML entities ★/☆. Line 312: clearing container (innerHTML=''). Line 600: hardcoded SVG markup for toolbar button icon.
color_picker_js.no_external_requests: No fetch/XMLHttpRequest/sendBeacon. All processing local — reads canvas pixels only.
color_picker_js.imports: bus, state from app.js; showToast from utils.js; registry from action-registry.js
color_picker_js.localStorage_usage: HISTORY_KEY ('color-picker-history') and FAVORITES_KEY ('color-picker-favorites') store hex color arrays. loadJSON() has try/catch fallback. MAX_HISTORY=20 bounds history array. Favorites array unbounded but user-driven (user clicks to add). Same-origin only.
color_picker_js.canvas_usage: Reads pixels via getImageData on existing pdf.js page canvases. Creates loupe canvas (120x120) for magnification preview. willReadFrequently hint set. No tainted canvas risk — same-origin canvases only.
color_picker_js.security_status: secure — no critical or medium issues found. 1 LOW-risk note (favorites array unbounded in localStorage).

known_issues: server_tokens set to 'build' - leaks nginx version (nginx/1.26.3 Ubuntu) in Server header. Should be 'off'.
No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. Any new .md file added to webroot would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed but no firewall enforces this.
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot.
CGI location block exists in nginx but no CGI scripts present - dead config that could be removed.
Codex proxy at /codex returns 502 - backend not running. No security risk but dead endpoint.

merge.js, batch.js, hyperlinks.js, bates.js, command-palette.js, keyboard-shortcuts.js, form-data.js, link-manager.js, reading-progress.js, bookmark-editor.js, layers.js use escapeHtml()/escHtml() that does not escape double quotes. CSP script-src 'self' mitigates inline handler injection. In most files used in element content (safe). In attribute contexts (title/aria-label/value/data-col) should add escapeAttr().

statistics.js esc() function (lines 448-452) does not escape double quotes — same pattern as escapeHtml() in other modules. Used in title attributes with PDF-derived font names (line 299) and image names (line 321). CSP mitigates. MEDIUM-LOW risk.

layers.js lines 505-507: OCG usage dictionary values (printState/viewState/exportState) from PDF inserted into innerHTML without escaping. A crafted PDF could inject HTML in the layer details popover. CSP blocks script execution. MEDIUM-LOW risk.
layers.js line 295,297,300: escapeHtml(group.name) in aria-label and title attributes — escapeHtml doesn't escape double quotes. Layer names from PDF OCG structure. CSP mitigates. LOW risk.
layers.js local downloadBlob() does not use sanitizeFilename from utils.js — download attribute prevents path traversal but inconsistent with other modules. LOW risk.
layers.js exportFlattenLayers: no processing timeout — renders all pages at 2x scale. Client-side self-DoS only for large PDFs. LOW risk.

bookmark-editor.js line 461: escapeHtml(item.title) in value attribute inside innerHTML template — escapeHtml doesn't escape double quotes. Bookmark titles from PDF outline or user input. CSP blocks inline handlers. Self-XSS only. LOW risk.
reading-progress.js line 387: bookmark name (bm.name) used unescaped in title attribute inside innerHTML. Self-XSS only — user sets own bookmark names, data in same-origin localStorage. LOW risk.
reading-progress.js line 550: escapeHtml(doc.name) in title attribute — escapeHtml doesn't escape double quotes. Self-XSS only — doc.name from user-selected file. LOW risk.

hyperlinks.js: detected PDF link URLs not validated for protocol scheme. A malicious PDF could contain javascript: URIs in link annotations. CSP + noopener/noreferrer mitigate. Should add protocol allowlist (http/https/mailto) before window.open().
hyperlinks.js line 686: email link window.open() missing noopener/noreferrer — minor since mailto: links are low risk.

context-menu.js line 643: window.open(ctx.url, '_blank', 'noopener') — opens PDF-derived URL without protocol allowlist. Same pattern as hyperlinks.js. A javascript: URI could execute if CSP does not block it. CSP script-src 'self' mitigates. MEDIUM-LOW risk.
img2pdf.js image validation uses OR logic (MIME type OR extension) - slightly permissive but acceptable for client-side processing.
insert-pages.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
metadata.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
batch.js line 294: escapeHtml() used with textContent causes double-escaping — cosmetic display bug for filenames containing &, <, >.
keyboard-shortcuts.js line 416: document.write used on new popup window for print cheat sheet — acceptable pattern but noted for tracking.
headers-footers.js: custom preset names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, only affects local storage size.
form-data.js: template names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, same pattern as headers-footers.js.
page-labels.js: custom preset names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, same pattern as headers-footers.js and form-data.js.

Session restore (TASK-036): restoreNotes/restoreAnnotations/restoreDrawings/restoreSignatures/restoreFormValues do not validate data schema from IndexedDB. IndexedDB is same-origin so risk is self-XSS only. Low priority.
link-manager.js Find & Replace: new RegExp() from user input could cause ReDoS with pathological patterns. Low impact — only runs against URL strings (typically short). Errors caught.
compare.js escapeHtml() does not escape double quotes — same pattern as other modules. Only used in element content context in HTML report, not in attributes. Safe.

snip.js line 865: innerHTML with template literal uses internally-generated values (pageNum, format, timestamp) — not user-controlled. LOW risk but should ideally use textContent/DOM API for defense-in-depth.
snip.js: no escapeHtml utility defined — acceptable since no user-controlled strings are inserted via innerHTML, but would be good practice to add.
snip.js: selections array unbounded — no max limit. Low risk since user-driven interaction.
snip.js line 661: raw err.message from clipboard API shown in toast — minor info disclosure, clipboard errors are browser-generated.
image-manager.js: no file size limit on replacement images — client-side self-DoS only, low risk.

verify-signatures.js: ASN.1 recursive parser (parseASN1) has no recursion depth limit — deeply nested crafted DER structure could cause stack overflow. LOW-MEDIUM risk. Input bounded by PKCS#7 Contents field.
verify-signatures.js: no upper bound on signature count parsed from ByteRange regex. Thousands of fake entries could slow UI rendering. LOW risk.

verify-signatures.js: byteRange values from PDF not bounds-checked against file length. Uint8Array.slice() handles gracefully (returns truncated array) but could produce incorrect hash comparisons silently. LOW risk.
verify-signatures.js: ASN.1 DER length field not validated against remaining data — parser continues with truncated view. LOW risk.

verify-signatures.js: escapeHtml() does not escape double quotes — same pattern as other modules. Used in title attributes at lines 1821, 1828 (batch verification). Self-XSS only — user selects own files. CSP blocks inline handlers. LOW risk.
verify-signatures.js: innerHTML += in loop (lines 1820, 1827) — inefficient DOM re-parsing per iteration. No direct security risk but poor practice. Should build string then assign once.

interleave.js: escapeHtml() DOM-based (best practice) but still doesn't escape double quotes. Used in title attribute at line 194. Self-XSS only — user selects own file. CSP blocks inline handlers. LOW risk.

annotation-presets.js lines 303, 309: active.name (user-editable preset name) inserted into innerHTML without escaping. Self-XSS only — user sets own preset names. CSP script-src 'self' blocks inline scripts. LOW risk.
annotation-presets.js line 507: escHtml(preset.name) in value attribute — escHtml DOM-based doesn't escape double quotes. Self-XSS only. CSP blocks inline handlers. LOW risk.
annotation-presets.js: preset names not length-limited in add()/update() — only importJSON() limits to 50 chars. HTML input maxlength=50 mitigates for UI edits. localStorage-only storage. LOW risk.
undo-redo.js: MEMORY_BUDGET_BYTES (50MB) defined but not enforced — only count-based trimming (MAX_HISTORY=100). Commands holding large page data could exceed memory budget. LOW risk for client-side.
undo-redo.js: escapeHtml() does not escape double quotes — same pattern as other modules. Only used in element content (cmd.description). SAFE.
page-resize.js: no processing timeout on applyResize() — client-side self-DoS only for very large PDFs. LOW risk.
bookmark-editor.js: outlineToTree() recursive with no explicit depth limit when loading PDF outlines. PDF outlines rarely exceed ~20 levels. LOW risk.
bookmark-editor.js: jsonToTree() recursive with no depth limit on imported JSON. User imports own files. LOW risk.
font-inspector.js: exportReportJSON() does not use sanitizeFilename from utils.js — download attribute prevents path traversal but inconsistent with other modules. LOW risk.
font-inspector.js: no explicit processing timeout on scanFonts() — has abortScan mechanism but no auto-timeout. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.

markdown-export.js line 992: innerHTML = simpleMarkdownToHtml(content) — simpleMarkdownToHtml escapes &<> (line 1012) but not double quotes. Link URLs from PDF annotations reach <a href> at line 1029. Double-quote in URL could break attribute. javascript: URIs possible. CSP script-src 'self' blocks both vectors. MEDIUM-LOW risk.

markdown-export.js lines 942-945: YAML frontmatter — PDF metadata Title/Author embedded with double quotes but not escaped. Quotes/newlines in metadata break YAML syntax in exported .md file. Data integrity issue, no direct XSS. LOW risk.
markdown-export.js: no explicit processing timeout on buildDocument(). Yields every BATCH_SIZE (10) pages via setTimeout. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.

bg-borders.js handleImageFile accepts image/svg+xml MIME type but applyBackgroundToPage only embeds PNG/JPEG via pdf-lib. SVG images render in canvas preview only (browser-sandboxed Image). SVG scripts/external refs blocked by browser when loaded as Image src. SAFE but slightly misleading UX — SVG backgrounds won't appear in final PDF output.
bg-borders.js no explicit processing timeout on applyBgBorders(). Yields every 5 pages. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
clipboard.js — no explicit blob size limit on clipboard images before processing. MAX_IMAGE_DIM=2000 downscaling mitigates. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
clipboard.js — pagePastes Map unbounded, no max paste item limit. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
clipboard.js — no processing timeout on processImageBlob() canvas conversion. LOW risk.
guides.js line 685 — drawRuler() infinite-form for loop. If tickPx = 0 (zoom = 0), loop never terminates and freezes UI. Should add guard: if (tickPx < 1) return; MEDIUM risk (client-side self-DoS).
guides.js — localStorage JSON.parse without schema validation. Same-origin only, falls back to defaults on error. LOW risk.
pdfa.js: sanitizeFilename imported but not called on download filename — downloadBlob from utils.js handles the download attribute. LOW risk.
pdfa.js: no processing timeout on runValidation() or convertToPdfA(). Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
annotation-exchange.js: no file size limit on XFDF import — XFDF files are text, typically small. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
annotation-exchange.js: parsePageRange unbounded Set growth from page range input — user-driven. LOW risk.
multiselect.js: selectedItems Map and namedGroups array unbounded — no max selection/group count. Client-side, user-driven only. LOW risk.
multiselect.js: group name from prompt() not length-limited — stored in memory only, not persisted. LOW risk.

epub-export.js line 591: fixed-layout <title>${chapter.title}</title> not XML-escaped. When chapterSplit='bookmarks', bookmark titles from PDF are raw. Creates potentially malformed XHTML in downloaded EPUB. Not web XSS. MEDIUM-LOW risk.
epub-export.js: double-escaping in reflow+headings path — heading text escaped at line 315, then again at line 629 in <title>. Cosmetic issue in EPUB. LOW risk.
epub-export.js line 336: flattenOutline() recursive with no depth limit. PDF outlines typically shallow. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
epub-export.js: no processing timeout on exportEpub(). Cover rendered at 1600px, pages at 150 DPI. Client-side self-DoS for large PDFs. LOW risk.
statistics.js countOutline() (line 199) recursive with no depth limit. PDF outlines typically shallow. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
statistics.js runAnalysis() has no auto-timeout. Has abort mechanism. Yields every 5 pages. Client-side self-DoS for large PDFs. LOW risk.
statistics.js export filenames (lines 429, 443) not passed through sanitizeFilename. downloadBlob from utils.js handles download attribute. LOW risk.
auto-crop.js: no processing timeout on analyzePages/applyAutoCrop. Yields every BATCH_SIZE (5) pages via requestIdleCallback. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
auto-crop.js: canvas elements in analyzePage() not explicitly released — GC handles cleanup. LOW risk for large batch analysis.
ocr.js: no auto-timeout on OCR extraction — has manual cancel button. Client-side self-DoS only for large PDFs with high DPI. LOW risk.
ocr.js: canvas elements in renderPageToCanvas() not explicitly released — GC handles. LOW risk.
smart-redact.js: custom regex from user input can cause ReDoS in worker. Worker isolation mitigates — main thread unaffected. LOW risk.
smart-redact.js: no auto-timeout on pattern scan. Worker terminated on completion/error. Client-side self-DoS only. LOW risk.
duplicate-detect.js: no processing timeout on startScan(). Uses BATCH_SIZE=20 with setTimeout yielding. Client-side self-DoS only for large PDFs. LOW risk.
duplicate-detect.js: canvas elements in processPage() not explicitly released — GC handles. LOW risk.
duplicate-detect.js: pageHashes array unbounded — grows with page count. Bounded by pdf.js page count limit. LOW risk.
color-picker.js: favorites array in localStorage unbounded — no max limit. User-driven (click to add). Same-origin only. LOW risk.

NOTE: smart-redact.js escapeHtml() (lines 454-458) uses DOM-based approach (createElement, textContent, innerHTML) — safest method, handles all chars including quotes. Best practice among all modules. Other modules should adopt this pattern.
NOTE: interleave.js escapeHtml() (lines 461-465) also uses DOM-based approach — same best practice as smart-redact.js.
NOTE: annotation-presets.js escHtml() (lines 779-783) also uses DOM-based approach — same best practice as smart-redact.js and interleave.js.
NOTE: ocr.js escapeHtml() (line 950) correctly escapes double quotes via string replacement — second-best practice.
notes: Security review completed — no critical fixes needed. 1 new file (TASK-090: color-picker.js). SECURE.

REVIEWED: color-picker.js (TASK-090) — PDF eyedropper color picker. 679 lines (new). Samples pixel colors from pdf.js page canvases, displays in HEX/RGB/HSL/CMYK with WCAG contrast ratios, magnifying loupe, history/favorites in localStorage. 5 innerHTML uses reviewed: all safe — hardcoded strings, HTML entities, integer values, and internally-generated color format strings only. No user-controlled strings in innerHTML. data-value attributes use color format strings (no double quotes possible). No eval/Function/document.write/window.open. No external requests. No file uploads/downloads. Uses canvas getImageData for pixel sampling (same-origin only). localStorage bounded for history (MAX_HISTORY=20), favorites unbounded but user-driven.
innerHTML count: 323 across 89 JS files (up from 318/88 — +5 from color-picker.js).
SSH: fail2ban total bans 8772 (+42 since last review). 3 IPs currently banned. Continued brute-force from 2.57.121.x subnet and new IPs. Generic usernames only. No successful unauthorized logins.
SSL certificate valid until June 18, 2026 (72 days remaining).
All security headers confirmed. Nginx blocking rules verified. Config syntax valid.
recommendations: MEDIUM: guides.js line 685 — add guard before drawRuler() tick loop: if (tickPx < 1) return; to prevent infinite loop when zoom approaches 0
MEDIUM-LOW: context-menu.js line 643 — add protocol allowlist before window.open(ctx.url): only allow http:, https:, mailto: schemes. Same fix needed in hyperlinks.js.
MEDIUM-LOW: statistics.js lines 299, 321 — esc() in title attributes with PDF-derived font/image names. Add double-quote escaping or use escAttr() for attribute contexts
MEDIUM-LOW: epub-export.js line 591 — escape chapter.title in fixed-layout <title>: use escapeXml(chapter.title)
MEDIUM-LOW: epub-export.js line 315/629 — avoid double-escaping: store raw text, escape only at output
MEDIUM-LOW: markdown-export.js line 1012 — add double-quote escaping to simpleMarkdownToHtml
MEDIUM-LOW: markdown-export.js line 1029 — add protocol allowlist for link URLs in generated <a href> tags
MEDIUM-LOW: markdown-export.js lines 942-945 — escape YAML special characters in frontmatter values
MEDIUM-LOW: Escape OCG usage values in layers.js lines 505-507
MEDIUM-LOW: Add protocol allowlist to hyperlinks.js handleLinkClick()
MEDIUM-LOW: verify-signatures.js lines 1820, 1827 — replace innerHTML += with string concatenation then single assignment for batch table
LOW: annotation-presets.js lines 303, 309 — escape active.name before inserting into innerHTML status display
LOW: Adopt smart-redact.js DOM-based escapeHtml pattern (createElement+textContent+innerHTML) across all modules — safest approach, handles all characters
LOW: statistics.js countOutline() — add recursion depth limit
LOW: epub-export.js line 336 — add recursion depth limit to flattenOutline()
LOW: verify-signatures.js parseASN1() — add recursion depth limit
LOW: verify-signatures.js — add upper bound cap on signature parsing
LOW: verify-signatures.js — add bounds validation on byteRange values
LOW: Add escapeAttr() to modules using escapeHtml()/esc() in attribute contexts
LOW: statistics.js — use sanitizeFilename from utils.js for export filenames
LOW: layers.js — use sanitizeFilename from utils.js in downloadBlob
LOW: font-inspector.js — use sanitizeFilename from utils.js in exportReportJSON
LOW: Fix batch.js line 294 — use fileName directly with textContent instead of escapeHtml(fileName)
LOW: Set server_tokens off in nginx.conf
LOW: Enable UFW firewall (sudo ufw allow 22,80,443/tcp && sudo ufw enable)
LOW: Add general *.md deny rule to nginx
LOW: Remove dead escapeHtml() from insert-pages.js and metadata.js
LOW: Remove unused CGI location block from nginx config
LOW: snip.js line 865 — replace innerHTML template literal with textContent/DOM API
LOW: Add file size limit in image-manager.js replaceImage()
LOW: undo-redo.js — enforce MEMORY_BUDGET_BYTES
LOW: bookmark-editor.js — add recursion depth limits to outlineToTree() and jsonToTree()
LOW: bg-borders.js — warn user when SVG selected (won't appear in PDF output)
LOW: clipboard.js — add max paste count limit
LOW: auto-crop.js — consider explicitly releasing canvas elements after each page analysis in batch mode
LOW: duplicate-detect.js — consider adding processing timeout for scan operations on very large PDFs
LOW: color-picker.js — consider adding max limit to favorites array in localStorage
